tender.title: Younger Adults' Supported Accommodation
tender.description:
The call-off will cover the care and support for Younger Adults Supported Accommodation Services commissioned by Hampshire County Council.  This could be from a few hours per week of shared support to 24/7 support with a team of support staff.  

The range care and support that may be required by individuals living in the Younger Adults Supported Accommodation includes: 

From prompting with personal care and daily living activities to full assistance of 1:1 or 2:1 support workers for all personal care and daily living activities 

Communication in a variety of different formats from fully verbal communication, through to the use of Makaton, BSL, and the full range of alternative communication methods such as pictures, symbols, objects of reference, PEC's 

From advice and guidance regarding managing and spending money to full support with managing money 

Support with managing behaviours which challenge resulting from a person's learning disability, mental health condition, traumatic history, unmet physical health needs, history of poor attachments or changes in medication 

From providing advice and guidance about living a healthy lifestyle to full support with all health needs and access to health services 

From advice and guidance about Community Activities to providing 1:1 or 2:1 support to access the Community 

Use of least restrictive approaches and positive behaviour support, although more direct interventions may be required 
 
Due to the number of lots and the expected interest this call-off is being run as a 2 stage process.  The first stage contained award questions that were generic across all lots and focused on how providers would address issues such as recruitment and retention.  Stage 2 featured case studies that were specific to situations the tenderers could expect to experience delivering the service.

tender.title: Social Housing White Paper Residents Survey - CONTRACT VARIATION
tender.description:
This contract has already been awarded and is published for transparency purposes only.

The Department for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities (DLUHC) wishes to commission a survey with social housing residents. This will support DLUHC in establishing a robust baseline position for the Social Housing White Paper programme from which we can set targets and monitor the progress of the programme. 

The purpose of this variation is to execute the option clause within the Contract to undertake Phase 2 to provide the final report and the presentation of findings and delivery of the dashboard.
